sources.label: Ashton, Shuster Bruce, Colledge, Dickinson (2015), 'The Search for MH370', Journal of Navigation 68(1), 1-22 (open access, Cambridge University Press)
Ashton et al. 2015 PDF (Table 9 re-extracted verbatim from PDF p.20 in this session; route description from pp.19, 21; conclusions p.22)
Ulich, Godfrey, Iannello, Banks (7 March 2020), 'The Final Resting Place of MH370' (UGIB 2020), via mh370.radiantphysics.com papers page
UGIB 2020 full paper PDF, 186 pages incl. Appendix E 'FMT Route' (downloaded and text-extracted in this session; source of all UGIB coordinates and times below)
OpenNav navigation database: waypoint coordinates BEDAX (5°21'51.58"N 93°47'15.27"E), IGOGU (7°31'01"N 94°25'00"E), ANOKO (7°08'10"N 94°24'58"E), fetched this session

Godfrey, Coetzee, Maskell (31 August 2023), 'MH370 Flight Path Analysis - Case Study', 232 pp (Table 5 'WSPR Anomalous Links and MH370 Positions', pp.50-53), PDF fetched 2026-07-16 via mh370search.com/2023/08/31/mh370-case-study/
Blelly, Marchand (CAPTIO, v4a 13 May 2022, upd. 24 June 2022), 'Analysis of Flight MH370 - From waypoint VAMPI until the estimated touch down', 21 pp (Tables 4, 9, 10), PDF fetched 2026-07-16

notes: Both reconstructions cover the SATCOM-only period (Epoch 3, ~18:25 UTC 7 Mar to 00:19 UTC 8 Mar 2014); no recorded positions exist in this window, so these are model-derived candidate paths, not measured tracks.

ashton-2015: all 13 rows of Table 9 'Example Reconstructed Flight Path Results' (PDF p.20) re-fetched 2026-07-16 and confirmed verbatim (times, lat, lon, track degETN, speed kph). The paper stresses this example route 'is no more valid than many other potential routes'. It crosses the 19:41 arc at 0N and flies south at a constant 450 kt ground speed; the route is illustrated in the paper's Figure 17 'Example Flight Path' (not Figs 13-15, which cover BFO modelling).

ashton-2015: Table 9 is SPARSE - it lists only the BFO calculation epochs. Straight polyline legs between consecutive rows are sparse-table interpolation, NOT the paper's route: (a) the 17:07:19 -> 18:25:27 leg straight-lines across the Malay Peninsula, whereas the actual radar-derived route was the IGARI turn-back over the peninsula near Penang and up the Strait of Malacca (paper Figs 1-2, 17); (b) the 18:39:58 -> 19:41:03 leg is described in section 6.3 as flying from the last radar fix to waypoint IGOGU (N07 31, E094 25), turning south towards waypoint ISBIX (N00 22, E093 40.5), 'with its true track drifting from the initial 186° (IGOGU to ISBIX) to 180° by the end of the flight'. ISBIX is included below as an un-timed anchor (it is a named waypoint with coordinates in the paper text, not a Table 9 row). No other points were added.

ugib-2020: rebuilt this session from the fetched 186-page paper. Every vertex is a published coordinate: Figure 3 event labels (p.4), Figure 4 arc labels (p.5), section 5.2.2 (p.18), Appendix E.1-E.3.9 (pp.127-137). The FMT route after 18:22:12 is: N571 past MEKAR/NILAM with a 15 NM right lateral offset, FLCH descent from 18:29:00, 'Direct To IGOGU' at FL250 (18:35:27, track 269.7 degT), 90-deg left turn at IGOGU to due south along the Kuala Lumpur/Chennai FIR boundary (passing ANOKO 18:46:21), 90-deg right turn at '6NFIR' (6N 94.4165E) to due west along the Chennai/Jakarta FIR boundary, 90-deg left turn at '6NFIRWEST' (6N 93.7875E) to BEDAX, then LNAV 180.0 degT from BEDAX toward the South Pole; ECON climb FL100->FL390 from 19:23:50; SIO route begins 19:41:03 at 2.9350N 93.7875E; LEP S34.2342 E93.7875 at 00:19:29 (pp.52, 57).

ugib-2020 synthesized/flagged items: (1) the IGOGU vertex has no published passage time - Appendix E.3.4 says the 90-deg turn began just after the first call ended (~18:41) and the descent ended 'about 2 minutes later' at 18:43:41 - so timeUtc is null there; (2) UGIB's FMT model computes bank-angle-limited turn arcs at 1-second resolution, while this polyline renders the four turns (IGOGU, 6NFIR, 6NFIRWEST, BEDAX) as sharp corners - the real modelled corners are rounded by a few NM; (3) the 18:53:46 closest approach to sailor Kate Tee and the 19:27:52 M300 airway crossing (FL212) are described in Appendix E but without published lat/lon rows, so they appear only in labels; (4) the 18:22:12 position is 6.547N 96.344E per Appendix E.1 text, while the Figure 3 label reads 6.578N 96.341E - the Appendix E value is used.

ugib-2020 longitude convention: the paper states the SIO route runs at constant longitude E93.7875 (BEDAX longitude, pp.128, 135); Figure 3/4 labels round this to 93.788E. The exact 93.7875 is used for every point on the BEDAX meridian. BEDAX latitude 5.3643N (p.163 and OpenNav 5°21'51.58"N agree). BEDAX passage time is 19:19:04 per Appendix E.3.8; main text p.25 says 'circa 19:19:16' - the Appendix E FMT-model value is used.
ugib-2020 ground speeds (groundSpeedKt) and tracks are the published Figure 3/Figure 4 annotations (knots, deg true).

A DSTG Bayesian / ATSB most-probable path was evaluated and excluded (checked again 2026-07-16 against the full Springer PDF, 124 pp): the book publishes the posterior distribution and most-probable paths only as raster figures (Figs 1.1, 10.1, 10.2) with no tabulated coordinates, so no memory-free vertices exist to plot.

wspr-gdtaaa-2023 (added 2026-07-16): all vertices are from Table 5 'WSPR Anomalous Links and MH370 Positions' (pp.50-53) of the fetched Godfrey/Coetzee/Maskell case-study PDF. The table is an embedded image; coordinates were transcribed from 200-dpi page renders in-session and spot-checked against the archive.org OCR mirror (first row 5.589117920N 99.165228400E and endpoint 29.128S 99.934E match). Rows with an anomalous link but no position fix are omitted. Treat as transcription-grade (typo risk on individual interior vertices), endpoint and arc-crossing rows double-checked.

wspr-gdtaaa-2023 methodology is CONTESTED: WSPR aircraft-scatter tracking is rejected by several MH370 analysts (Victor Iannello, Duncan Steel and others) and is not peer-reviewed; the 29.1S endpoint also contradicts the CSIRO drift and Boeing end-of-flight work favouring 33-36S. Included as a notable, clearly-flagged alternative hypothesis, not as an endorsed track. Its rows before 18:22:12 (18:01-18:14) overlap the recorded Epoch 2 window and are sliced off at build time.

captio-2022 (added 2026-07-16): vertices are the decimal-degree values printed in Tables 4 and 9 of the fetched CAPTIO v4a PDF; times converted from MYT (UTC+8) as printed. Known source typo: the 18:40 phone-call point is printed 94°37'37.44"E in Table 4 vs 94°34'37.44"E in Table 9, but the decimal 94.5771 is consistent in both and is used. The post-glide Point of Impact 35°39'S 93°01'E (Executive Summary p.1) is an un-timed terminal vertex. The CAPTIO endpoint region was substantially covered by the failed Ocean Infinity 2025-26 search.

citation.label: Ashton et al. (2015), 'The Search for MH370', Journal of Navigation 68(1), Table 9 'Example Reconstructed Flight Path Results', p.20; route description section 6.3, p.21

points.label: Table 9 row 1: pre-departure at Kuala Lumpur gate

Table 9 row 4: last ACARS-era point. LEG TO NEXT POINT IS SPARSE-TABLE INTERPOLATION: the actual route was the IGARI turn-back across the Malay Peninsula and up the Strait of Malacca (paper Figs 1-2, 17)
Table 9 row 5: 02:25 (UTC+8) arc crossing, consistent with airway N571 towards IGOGU at 480 kt (paper p.19)

Table 9 row 6: first phone call epoch, at/near waypoint IGOGU (N07 31, E094 25, paper p.19). LEG TO NEXT POINT: section 6.3 route turns south at IGOGU towards ISBIX at initial track 186 degT; Table 9 has no intermediate rows (sparse-table, see paper Fig 17)
Waypoint ISBIX (N00 22, E093 40.5; coordinates from paper section 6.3, p.21) - named turn-toward waypoint, not a Table 9 row
Table 9 row 11: second phone call epoch
Table 9 row 13: 08:19 (UTC+8) arc - paper conclusions: 'a last contact location of 34.7°S and 93.0°E', with 'significant uncertainty in the final location' (p.22)

citation.label: Ulich, Godfrey, Iannello, Banks (7 March 2020), 'The Final Resting Place of MH370', Figs 3-4 (pp.4-5), section 5.2 (p.18), Appendix E 'FMT Route' (pp.127-137)

parameters.description: FMT route N571 -> IGOGU -> FIR boundaries -> BEDAX at FL100, then great-circle LNAV route at exactly 180.0 deg true from waypoint BEDAX (5.3643 N, 93.7875 E) toward the South Pole, FL390, LRC speed; constant longitude E93.7875; intersects the 7th arc (last estimated position, LEP) at S34.2342 E93.7875.

points.label: Last military radar fix, 10 NM past waypoint MEKAR on N571, FL385, LRC (App. E.1 p.127; Fig. 3 label reads 6.578N 96.341E)
SATCOM power-up, 20.5 NM beyond MEKAR (p.18); ~20 s later a 15 NM right lateral offset from N571 began, between MEKAR and NILAM (App. E.3.1)
Start of FLCH descent FL385 -> FL100 at 250 KIAS (Fig. 3; App. E.3.2)
At FL250: 'Direct To IGOGU' executed, track 296.1 -> 269.7 degT, lateral offset cancelled (Fig. 3; App. E.3.3)
First SATCOM phone call starts, FL163, still descending (Fig. 3)

Waypoint IGOGU (7°31'01"N 94°25'00"E, OpenNav; Ashton et al. p.19 agrees): 90-deg LNAV left turn to due south along the Kuala Lumpur/Chennai FIR boundary, begun just after the call ended, ~02:41 (UTC+8)-02:43 (UTC+8) (App. E.3.4) - no exact passage time published
End of descent at FL100, between IGOGU and ANOKO; slowed to holding speed 217 KIAS, reached 02:44:07 (UTC+8) (Fig. 3; App. E.3.4)
Waypoint ANOKO (7°08'10"N 94°24'58"E, OpenNav), passed southbound at FL100, 217 KIAS (App. E.3.5); closest approach to sailor Kate Tee 02:53:46 (UTC+8), 1.7 NM (App. E.3.5)
'6NFIR' (6N 94.4165E, App. E.3.6): 90-deg right turn from due south to due west along the Chennai/Jakarta FIR boundary
'6NFIRWEST' (6N 93.7875E, App. E.3.7): 90-deg left turn direct to waypoint BEDAX

Waypoint BEDAX (5°21'51.58"N 93°47'15.27"E, OpenNav; UGIB 5.3643N), passed at FL100 (App. E.3.8; main text p.25: circa 03:19:16 (UTC+8)). South Pole waypoint entered after BEDAX -> LNAV track exactly 180.0 degT until fuel exhaustion
Start of ECON climb FL100 -> FL390 (Fig. 3; App. E.3.9); airway M300 crossed 03:27:52 (UTC+8) at FL212
2nd arc - SIO route begins: 2.9350N 93.7875E, FL390, LRC (App. E.2 p.128; Fig. 4)
7th arc - Last Estimated Position S34.2342 E93.7875 (pp.52, 57); FL192, descending 4,304 fpm, fuel exhausted (Fig. 4)

citation.label: Godfrey, Coetzee, Maskell (31 Aug 2023), 'MH370 Flight Path Analysis - Case Study', Table 5 pp.50-53; prime crash location p.5/p.49. Methodology contested (not peer-reviewed; rejected by Iannello, Steel and others)

points.label: Table 5 row 1: last known position (overlaps recorded Epoch 2; sliced at build)
First WSPR position after the 02:22:12 (UTC+8) last radar fix
Final WSPR position = prime crash location (29.128S 99.934E), 44.27 NM from the 7th arc

citation.label: Blelly & Marchand (CAPTIO v4a, May/June 2022), 'Analysis of Flight MH370 - From waypoint VAMPI until the estimated touch down', Tables 4, 9, 10; Executive Summary p.1

points.label: VAMPI, last precisely known location (Table 4; overlaps recorded Epoch 2; sliced at build)
First unanswered SATCOM phone call; start of the constant Mach 0.706 southern leg (Tables 4, 9)
Last powered point; piloted glide follows (Table 9)
Northernmost probable Point of Impact after glide and controlled ditching, 35°39'S 93°01'E (Executive Summary p.1)
